message:
fix(limits): cap pattern-pack loader and wa.send payload sizes (ft-05hfm)

Closes ft-05hfm. Two defense-in-depth caps on user-controlled byte
streams reaching in-process buffers:

## F1 — pattern-pack loader cap

`load_pack_from_file` at patterns.rs:978 previously used
`std::fs::read_to_string` with no size cap, then fed the bytes to
`serde_yaml::from_str` / `from_str` / `toml::from_str` with no
depth cap. A hostile `.ft/patterns/*.yaml` in a cloned repo could
exhaust the allocator (multi-GiB file into memory) or stack-blow
the YAML deserializer on deeply-nested constructs.

Fix:
- New constant `MAX_PACK_BYTES: u64 = 1 MiB`. Three orders of
  magnitude above the biggest built-in pack (`builtin_claude_code_
  pack` serializes to ~10 KiB) so legitimate packs have ample
  headroom.
- `std::fs::metadata(&resolved)` stat-check rejects oversize
  packs BEFORE `read_to_string` buffers anything. Cheaper than
  inhaling the full file first.
- Second-line bytes-read check catches filesystems that misreport
  size (procfs-like pseudo files) or TOCTOU swap.
- Structured error via `PatternError::InvalidRule` cites the
  observed size, the cap, and the `ft-05hfm` tag so operator
  triage knows which audit the bound came from.

## F2 — wa.send text payload cap

`WaSendTool::call` at mcp_tools.rs:1819 previously accepted a
`params.text` string of any length. An MCP client could submit a
multi-gigabyte payload; the full string would transit the
injector → policy → `wezterm cli send-text` pipeline before
anyone noticed, OOM-ing the watcher.

Fix:
- New constant `MAX_SEND_TEXT_BYTES: usize = 1 MiB`. ~250x the
  largest legitimate paste (full Claude Code prompt + context).
- Length check added immediately after the existing `timeout_secs
  == 0` guard so the rejection fires before any blocking runtime
  setup, storage open, pane lookup, or policy evaluation.
- Envelope carries `MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS` with the observed size,
  the cap, and a hint pointing operators at split-payload
  workflows.

## Tests (6 new)

patterns.rs:
- `load_pack_from_file_rejects_oversize_yaml` — hostile
  `huge.yaml` of `MAX_PACK_BYTES + 64` bytes must reject with a
  message citing `max allowed` and `MAX_PACK_BYTES`.
- `load_pack_from_file_accepts_pack_at_cap_boundary` — a pack
  padded with YAML comment bytes to land just under the cap
  must parse successfully; regression guard against over-
  tightening the bound.
- `load_pack_from_file_rejects_oversize_json` — the same bound
  applies to JSON pack files.

mcp_tools.rs:
- `max_send_text_bytes_is_one_mib` — pin the constant value so
  accidental bumps surface as an explicit test update.
- `wa_send_schema_declares_text_field` — regression guard that
  the `text` schema field still exists; the cap is only
  meaningful if the field it protects hasn't been renamed.
- `max_send_text_bytes_exceeds_typical_paste` — boundary-sanity
  test: the cap must leave headroom above a 100 KiB typical
  paste so legitimate usage doesn't regress.

Full `WaSendTool::call` end-to-end test deferred: constructing a
real `McpContext` + wezterm-handle + runtime would be a larger
harness than the 25-min cap allows. The unit coverage pins the
cap, the schema field, and the boundary semantics; the call-path
wiring is three lines of direct `if` check that any reviewer can
eyeball.

## Non-changes

- On-disk format unchanged. No schema migration, no serialized
  format drift.
- Public API unchanged. `MAX_PACK_BYTES` and `MAX_SEND_TEXT_BYTES`
  are `pub(crate)` so in-tree callers can reference them, but
  they're not part of the stable surface.
- No new dependency. Uses only std (`fs::metadata`, `String::len`).

## Related

- ft-xv561 (070117a0) — same attack class (hostile pattern pack
  → DoS), different primitive (regex backtrack vs YAML parse).
- ft-kegvt (969b9e02) — restricted-input family: user-supplied
  strings flowing into a trusted sink without a structural cap.

Audit tally now 7 shipped fixes across 8 passes:
  30988e53 CRITICAL MCP path traversal
  070117a0 HIGH pattern ReDoS
  969b9e02 MEDIUM restore-command CR/LF sanitizer
  598a7c13 MEDIUM snapshot integrity hash
  e790f481 LOW diag cwd privacy
  0f38972b LOW mux-server SIGTERM handler
  [this] LOW/MEDIUM pack + send size caps
